tickets.title: Laptop Not Powering On
tickets.body: The user reported that their company-issued Dell Latitude laptop is completely unresponsive and does not power on. The charging indicator light does not appear even after the device has been connected to the official charger for more than two hours. The user attempted several troubleshooting steps including holding the power button for 30 seconds, trying a different power outlet, and reconnecting the charging cable, but the issue persists. The laptop was functioning normally the previous evening before suddenly shutting down during regular use. This may indicate a hardware-related problem involving the battery, charging port, or motherboard components.

tickets.title: Email Client Crashing Frequently
tickets.body: The user is experiencing repeated crashes in Microsoft Outlook whenever attempting to open or download email attachments larger than 5MB. According to the user, the issue began shortly after a recent Windows and Office software update was installed on the system. Smaller attachments open normally, but larger files cause Outlook to freeze for several seconds before closing unexpectedly. The crashes are disrupting daily communication and preventing access to important client documents. Initial troubleshooting such as restarting the application and rebooting the system has not resolved the problem.

tickets.title: Printer Not Responding
tickets.body: Multiple employees on the 3rd floor have reported that the shared office printer is not responding to any print requests. The printer currently displays an offline status on connected workstations even though it appears powered on. Users have attempted to resend print jobs and reconnect to the network printer without success. The issue started earlier this morning and has impacted several departments that rely on the device for printing reports and invoices. Possible causes may include a network connectivity issue, outdated printer drivers, or a hardware malfunction within the printer itself.

tickets.title: Blue Screen Error on Startup
tickets.body: The user’s Windows workstation is encountering a blue screen error immediately during startup, preventing access to the operating system. The displayed stop code is MEMORY_MANAGEMENT, which may indicate faulty RAM, corrupted system files, or driver-related conflicts. The issue began after the system unexpectedly restarted during normal office work. Multiple reboot attempts result in the same error message, and the user is unable to enter the desktop environment. This issue is currently blocking the employee from performing work tasks and may require advanced diagnostics or hardware replacement.

tickets.title: VPN Connection Failure
tickets.body: A remote employee reported being unable to establish a connection to the company VPN after recently completing a password reset. Each login attempt results in an authentication failure message despite the user confirming the new password is correct and functioning for other company services such as email and internal portals. The issue is preventing secure remote access to company resources and internal applications required for daily operations. The user has already restarted the device and reinstalled the VPN client, but the problem continues to occur.

tickets.title: Software License Activation Issue
tickets.body: The user is unable to activate an Adobe Creative Cloud license on a newly assigned workstation despite having a valid and active company subscription. During the activation process, the software displays an error stating that the license cannot be verified or assigned to the device. The user previously used the license on an older machine that has since been decommissioned. Attempts to sign out and back into the Adobe account have not resolved the issue. This problem is delaying access to critical design tools needed for ongoing project work.

tickets.title: Keyboard Keys Not Working
tickets.body: The user reported that several keys on their external keyboard, particularly the spacebar and enter key, have become intermittently unresponsive during use. The issue started suddenly during regular work activity and is affecting typing accuracy and productivity. The user attempted reconnecting the keyboard, testing different USB ports, and restarting the computer, but the affected keys still fail to register consistently. No visible physical damage is present, although the user mentioned a minor liquid spill may have occurred near the keyboard earlier in the week.

tickets.title: Slow System Performance
tickets.body: The user’s desktop computer has been experiencing significantly reduced performance, especially when running multiple applications simultaneously. Common tasks such as opening spreadsheets, browsing the web, and switching between programs are taking much longer than usual. The user also reported occasional freezing and delayed response times when accessing shared network drives. The issue has gradually worsened over the past two weeks and is affecting overall productivity. Potential causes may include insufficient system memory, high background process usage, outdated hardware, or malware-related activity.

tickets.title: Unable to Install Security Update
tickets.body: The user is unable to successfully install the latest Windows security update on their workstation. Every installation attempt fails with error code 0x80070005, which generally indicates a permissions or access-related problem. The user has attempted the update multiple times over the past several days, including after restarting the system, but the installation continues to fail. This issue leaves the workstation without the latest security patches and may expose the system to potential vulnerabilities. Further investigation into system permissions, antivirus conflicts, or corrupted update files may be required.

tickets.title: Monitor Flickering Issue
tickets.body: The user reported that an external monitor connected through HDMI intermittently flickers during normal operation. The screen briefly goes black for a few seconds before returning to normal display. The issue occurs randomly throughout the day and becomes more noticeable when running graphics-intensive applications or during video calls. The user has already checked cable connections and restarted both the monitor and workstation, but the flickering continues. Possible causes could include a faulty HDMI cable, outdated graphics drivers, refresh rate mismatch, or monitor hardware issues.
